text.title: sql-optimization Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for sql-optimization as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Trusting EXPLAIN estimates without running ANALYZE on production-sized data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Estimates can be wrong by orders of magnitude if table statistics are stale or data distribution differs from estimate.

failure-mode.description: Adding indexes speculatively "in case they help" without profiling queries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unused indexes slow writes and consume disk space without benefit. Every index adds cost to inserts, updates, deletes. Add indexes only for queries that are actually slow.

failure-mode.description: SELECT * instead of selecting specific columns.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fetches unnecessary columns, increases network bandwidth, may prevent index-only scans. Always specify needed columns.

failure-mode.description: N+1 queries: one query returns rows, then a loop runs individual queries for each row.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Causes many network round-trips and transaction overhead. Use JOIN with GROUP BY or batch loading instead.

failure-mode.description: Missing WHERE clause on UPDATE or DELETE operations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Updates or deletes the entire table. Always include WHERE to limit scope. Consider soft deletes or archiving instead.

failure-mode.description: Implicit type conversion in WHERE clause causes index bypass.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: WHERE varchar_col = 123 converts 123 to string, preventing index use. Use correct types in queries and applications.

failure-mode.description: LIKE '%pattern%' for substring search on large tables.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Full-text search is needed. LIKE with leading wildcard cannot use index and scans the entire table.

failure-mode.description: Using DISTINCT as a band-aid for JOIN problems.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DISTINCT hides the real problem: wrong join or missing WHERE. Fix the join instead.

failure-mode.description: ORDER BY RAND() to shuffle results on large tables.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Extremely slow; RAND() must be evaluated for every row and the result set must be sorted. Use OFFSET/LIMIT with a random starting point instead.

failure-mode.description: Not using prepared statements or parameterized queries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Security risk (SQL injection) and performance issue (query plan not cached). Always parameterize.
